activities_projects.title: Hydrological Cycle Diagram
activities_projects.objective: Understand how water circulates in nature
steps: Draw a diagram of the hydrological cycle
Label evaporation, condensation, precipitation, and runoff
Explain how water gets renewed in this cycle
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand why water is a renewable resource
activities_projects.evaluation: Accuracy and neatness of diagram (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Identify Causes of Water Scarcity
steps: List five causes of water scarcity
Explain each cause in one or two sentences
Give an example from your region if possible
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand factors responsible for water scarcity

activities_projects.title: Map Work – Major Rivers and Dams
activities_projects.objective: Identify important rivers and dams in India
steps: Mark major rivers of India on the map
Locate dams such as Bhakra Nangal, Hirakud, and Sardar Sarovar
Label the rivers and dams clearly
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students learn geographical locations of major water projects

activities_projects.title: Advantages of Dams
activities_projects.objective: Understand the uses of multipurpose river projects
steps: Write five benefits of dams
Explain how dams help in irrigation and electricity generation
Give one example of a multipurpose project
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand the importance of dams

activities_projects.title: Disadvantages of Large Dams
steps: List four problems caused by large dams
Explain how dams affect rivers and people
Write one possible solution
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students analyze environmental effects of dams

activities_projects.title: Rainwater Harvesting Model
activities_projects.objective: Understand the concept of rooftop rainwater harvesting
steps: Draw a simple model of rooftop rainwater harvesting
Show pipes collecting water from roofs
Show storage tanks or underground tanks
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students visualize how rainwater harvesting works

activities_projects.title: Traditional Water Harvesting Methods
activities_projects.objective: Learn traditional techniques of water conservation
steps: Write about three traditional water harvesting methods
Examples: tankas, johads, kuls, bamboo drip irrigation
Explain where these methods are used
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students learn traditional water conservation practices

activities_projects.title: Water Usage Survey
steps: Observe water usage in your home for one day
List activities such as drinking, cooking, washing, bathing
Estimate the amount of water used
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand water usage in daily life

activities_projects.title: Poster on Water Conservation
steps: Create a poster with the message 'Save Water'
Include slogans and drawings
Highlight methods to conserve water
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students develop awareness about water conservation

activities_projects.title: Water Dispute Role Play
steps: Divide students into groups representing different states
Discuss how water from a river should be shared
Perform a short role play showing negotiation
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand conflicts over water resources
